semantic_text:
can: bcm: Clear bo->bcm_proc_read after remove_proc_entry().

[ Upstream commit 94b0818fa63555a65f6ba107080659ea6bcca63e ]

syzbot reported a warning in bcm_release(). [0]

The blamed change fixed another warning that is triggered when
connect() is issued again for a socket whose connect()ed device has
been unregistered.

However, if the socket is just close()d without the 2nd connect(), the
remaining bo->bcm_proc_read triggers unnecessary remove_proc_entry()
in bcm_release().

Let's clear bo->bcm_proc_read after remove_proc_entry() in bcm_notify().

[0]

cpufreq: ti-cpufreq: Introduce quirks to handle syscon fails appropriately

[ Upstream commit abc00ffda43bd4ba85896713464c7510c39f8165 ]

Commit b4bc9f9e27ed ("cpufreq: ti-cpufreq: add support for omap34xx
and omap36xx") introduced special handling for OMAP3 class devices
where syscon node may not be present. However, this also creates a bug
where the syscon node is present, however the offset used to read
is beyond the syscon defined range.

Fix this by providing a quirk option that is populated when such
special handling is required. This allows proper failure for all other
platforms when the syscon node and efuse offsets are mismatched.

Fixes: b4bc9f9e27ed ("cpufreq: ti-cpufreq: add support for omap34xx and omap36xx")
Signed-off-by: Nishanth Menon <nm@ti.com>
Tested-by: Dhruva Gole <d-gole@ti.com>
Reviewed-by: Kevin Hilman <khilman@baylibre.com>
Signed-off-by: Viresh Kumar <viresh.kumar@linaro.org>
Signed-off-by: Sasha Levin <sashal@kernel.org>

can: m_can: m_can_close(): stop clocks after device has been shut down

[ Upstream commit 2c09b50efcad985cf920ca88baa9aa52b1999dcc ]

After calling m_can_stop() an interrupt may be pending or NAPI might
still be executed. This means the driver might still touch registers
of the IP core after the clocks have been disabled. This is not good
practice and might lead to aborts depending on the SoC integration.

To avoid these potential problems, make m_can_close() symmetric to
m_can_open(), i.e. stop the clocks at the end, right before shutting
down the transceiver.

Fixes: e0d1f4816f2a ("can: m_can: add Bosch M_CAN controller support")
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20240910-can-m_can-fix-ifup-v3-2-6c1720ba45ce@pengutronix.de
Signed-off-by: Marc Kleine-Budde <mkl@pengutronix.de>
Signed-off-by: Sasha Levin <sashal@kernel.org>

qcacmn: Add connected_beacon_interval subfilter

This change adds a subfilter to local packet capture feature, allowing
the user to specify a specific interval at which they would like to
receive beacon packets.

Change-Id: I1c2a3e568d2ab35c49dbe733a7edbd32f3bb7ff3
CRs-Fixed: 4046913

msm: update offset value of keyslot in derive raw secret call

In program key call flow, keyslot offset calculation is skipped if
"hwkm_init_done" flag is set to true. In case of neo_le hwkm_init_done
flag is set to true in derive raw secret call. So, in program key call
flow the offset calculation is skipped. To fix that offset calculation
 is also included in derive raw secret call flow.

Test: Able to read and write files after reboot in
overlay/encrypted_dir.

Change-Id: I3e324a4cd9aaade90e4c628912f859f01c689365
Signed-off-by: Debraj Mukhopadhyay <quic_dmukhopa@quicinc.com>

Release 5.2.1.66D

Release 5.2.1.66D

Change-Id: I5c6f2e2d1013f71b4c16947d6b318aa9935d8ce1
CRs-Fixed: 774533

Bluetooth: btusb: Fix not handling ZPL/short-transfer

[ Upstream commit 7b05933340f4490ef5b09e84d644d12484b05fdf ]

Requesting transfers of the exact same size of wMaxPacketSize may result
in ZPL/short-transfer since the USB stack cannot handle it as we are
limiting the buffer size to be the same as wMaxPacketSize.

Also, in terms of throughput this change has the same effect to
interrupt endpoint as 290ba200815f "Bluetooth: Improve USB driver throughput
by increasing the frame size" had for the bulk endpoint, so users of the
advertisement bearer (e.g. BT Mesh) may benefit from this change.

Fixes: 5e23b923da03 ("[Bluetooth] Add generic driver for Bluetooth USB devices")
Signed-off-by: Luiz Augusto von Dentz <luiz.von.dentz@intel.com>
Tested-by: Kiran K <kiran.k@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Sasha Levin <sashal@kernel.org>

dt-bindings: arm: qcom,ids: Add SoC ID for ParrotPRO variants

Add soc id for ParrotPRO P and Q variants.

Change-Id: I808698215f72efa3cd3b2642d6156cf029dc4dc3
Signed-off-by: Saranya R <quic_sarar@quicinc.com>
(cherry picked from commit 2f3aee19cbb5268e30b42c5a1ebf1183f406a234)

qcacld-3.0: Avoid phymode and puncture mismatch

Host semd wmi as following order after CSA received/vdev restart and
before vdev up, old puncture bitmap mismatched with new phy mode lead to
F/W assert.
1.	WMI PEER PHY MODE
2.	WMI PEER PUNCTURE BITMAP and BW

To fix it, swap order as following
1.	WMI PEER PUNCTURE BITMAP and BW
2.	WMI PEER PHY MODE

Change-Id: I1ae3e5093cb45520be0f50ffb31fa7386201340b
CRs-Fixed: 3650797

msm: eva: Validating the SFR buffer size before accessing

To avoid any OOB write or other security issues,it's good to
validate the buffer size before accessing it.

Change-Id: Ibfdef21293c9385119cfb6338ef36e20c0fc1f2f
Signed-off-by: Pulkit Singh Tak <quic_ptak@quicinc.com>

msm: npu: Fix use after free issue

There is possibility that network will be used after free.
This change is to fix this issue.

Change-Id: I12205b750450bee36f85dff3f620f8f0689a4e46
Signed-off-by: Gao Wang <quic_gaowang@quicinc.com>

qcacmn: Modify RF test mode scan and connection policy

For RF test mode, when the power type value is set to 1 or 2,
the host checks if a connection is possible in SP and VLP power
modes, respectively. If not, the host checks for a possible
connection in LPI power mode.

With the updated requirements, for RF test mode, when the power
type value is set to 1 or 2, scan and connect to the AP only if
a connection is possible in SP and VLP power modes, respectively.
Additionally, do not connect to the AP if its power type does
not match the RF test mode force power type.

Change-Id: Ic900aa17569a4542362db3ce3341d38379888dd2
CRs-Fixed: 4021160

x86/sgx: Fix deadlock in SGX NUMA node search

[ Upstream commit 9c936844010466535bd46ea4ce4656ef17653644 ]

When the current node doesn't have an EPC section configured by firmware
and all other EPC sections are used up, CPU can get stuck inside the
while loop that looks for an available EPC page from remote nodes
indefinitely, leading to a soft lockup. Note how nid_of_current will
never be equal to nid in that while loop because nid_of_current is not
set in sgx_numa_mask.

Also worth mentioning is that it's perfectly fine for the firmware not
to setup an EPC section on a node. While setting up an EPC section on
each node can enhance performance, it is not a requirement for
functionality.

Rework the loop to start and end on *a* node that has SGX memory. This
avoids the deadlock looking for the current SGX-lacking node to show up
in the loop when it never will.

Fixes: 901ddbb9ecf5 ("x86/sgx: Add a basic NUMA allocation scheme to sgx_alloc_epc_page()")
Reported-by: "Molina Sabido, Gerardo" <gerardo.molina.sabido@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Aaron Lu <aaron.lu@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Dave Hansen <dave.hansen@linux.intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Kai Huang <kai.huang@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jarkko Sakkinen <jarkko@kernel.org>
Acked-by: Dave Hansen <dave.hansen@linux.intel.com>
Tested-by: Zhimin Luo <zhimin.luo@intel.com>
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20240905080855.1699814-2-aaron.lu%40intel.com
Signed-off-by: Sasha Levin <sashal@kernel.org>

qcacmn: Added null ptr check for dev ctx

In the mlo mgr link switch complete,
it appears that the dev ctx referenced by
vdev could potentially be null, leading to
a null pointer dereference. To prevent
this, a null pointer check has been added.

Change-Id: Ib81f23d15ac5b9fa045ec9944bd07989eec07e61
CRs-Fixed: 3992083
Merge "soc: qcom: Add SoC ID for ParrotPRO variants" into kernel.lnx.6.6.r1-rel
Merge "soc: qcom: Add SCT table for neo le"

qcacmn: Add tx desc sanity check

Tx desc found through invalid sw cookie
causes null pointer dereference.To avoid such situation
check for sanity of tx desc before processing it.

Compare paddr received in tx completion desc with paddr of
tx desc found through sw cookie.

Change-Id: I9ba9dbe4d8639edb1adf5c996c1ddf3828c2be4a
CRs-Fixed: 4113009

qcacmn: remove QDF_ASSERT warning for htc packets sending

If WMI cmd sending and runtime PM suspending happened at same time,
there is chance get_htc_send_packets_credit_based() report warning
to dmesg, this warning info is not much helpful.

Use one line print instead to avoid confusion.

Change-Id: I95efd9514063d05a55959a3d8b17fecb7bbd75e1
CRs-Fixed: 4071193

power: supply: qbg: Add 32-bit compat ioctl support

When a 32-bit userspace entity calls a IOCTL call on 64-bit Kernel
there can be case where the IOCTL number between user-space and
kernel to be calculated differently because of different type size.

Add support for compat IOCTL which converts the user-space IOCTL
number to kernel supported IOCTL number to fix the above issue.

Also, change the datatype from unsigned long to __u64 for some
struct attributes as the datatype size is calculated differently
in user-space and kernel space.

Change-Id: Idd979b019fe62885038999a5a7d190b253acab83
Signed-off-by: Umang Chheda <quic_uchheda@quicinc.com>
Signed-off-by: Guang Yang <quic_guyang@quicinc.com>

power: supply: qti-qbg: Fix QBG_DATA_FULL_IRQ race condition

If the QBG SDAM FIFO fills up before IRQ registration, the qbg_data_full
irq won't be acted upon, and so the FIFO never gets cleared,
and the PMIC won't raise the interrupt again.

To fix this, disable the interrupt before registering the IRQ handler,
then re-enable the interrupt post interrupt handler registration.
This toggling ensures the PMIC raises the interrupt if FIFO is
currently full, no matter when it ended up filling up. Furthermore,
this approach keeps the IRQ handler as the only piece of code that
clears the FIFO after IRQ registration, avoiding concurrency concerns.

Change-Id: I2d80cffcdd415ab9058a1c7f9d36043e8ee5e1c5
Signed-off-by: Umang Chheda <quic_uchheda@quicinc.com>
